fields.narrative_intro: *A neurotic person and a punk person discuss the rsync AI bug scandal in a doomsday bunker.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
NEUROTIC PERSON: "Claude destroyed rsync! v3.4.3 caused a 329-comment meltdown!"
fields.panel2:
[build]
PUNK PERSON: "Did you check v3.4.1? No AI. 59 bugs. Nine commits."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
NEUROTIC PERSON: "That's... 39.39 severity per ten commits. The worst ever."
PUNK PERSON: "Zero outrage. Zero."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The neurotic person's eyes slowly slide sideways toward a wall of printed rsync changelogs taped in a conspiracy web. A single string of red yarn connects v3.4.1 to a sticky note reading 'NO AI TO BLAME'. A bunker emergency light blinks overhead.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
NEUROTIC PERSON: "So the p-value was 46%. It was a coin flip the whole time?"
PUNK PERSON: "The AI scored the bugs. To prove AI's fine."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The neurotic person stares at a framed print of the permutation test graph on the bunker wall. They've added a sticky note: 'Nobody noticed because there was no AI to be angry at.' Below it, a smaller sticky: 'I noticed.' Below that, even smaller: 'I am so angry.']
NEUROTIC PERSON: "I need more yarn."
fields.narrative_summary: The joke is that the anti-AI rsync outrage collapses under its own statistics — the worst release had zero AI involvement, the p-value was a coin flip, and the only thing proven is that humans need a villain, not a bug count.

narrative_summary: The joke is that the anti-AI rsync outrage collapses under its own statistics — the worst release had zero AI involvement, the p-value was a coin flip, and the only thing proven is that humans need a villain, not a bug count.
rationale: Candidate 5 earns its win through the escalating sticky-note button — 'I noticed. I am so angry.' — which is a genuinely earned visual gag that doesn't just restate the setup but adds a new layer of self-aware absurdity. The conspiracy-yarn visual in panel 4 is the best use of the bunker setting across all five candidates, and 'I need more yarn' is a clean, specific closer that lands harder than any of the competitors' final lines.
story_title: Did Claude increase bugs in rsync?
